claim: Fox News is unavailable in Canada because the Radio Act makes it a crime to lie to the public via airwaves.
label: Refuted
justification: The answers and sources clearly show that Fox news is available in Canada so the claim is refuted.

questions.question: Does Canada have a Radio Act?

answers.answer: No
answers.boolean_explanation: It has the Canadian Radio-televison and Telecommunications Commission Act.

questions.question: Is Fox an approved broadcaster under the CRTC?

answers.answer: Yes
answers.boolean_explanation:
CBC News · Posted: Nov 18, 2004 11:17 AM ET | Last Updated: November 19, 2004
The conservative-leaning Fox News Channel will soon be coming to Canadian digital television channels.

The Canadian Radio-television and Telecommunications Commission (CRTC) approved an application Thursday to bring the Fox News Channel, one of the highest-rated news channels in the United States, onto Canadian digital airwaves.

answers.answer: Yes
answers.boolean_explanation:
FOX News Channel is currently available internationally via the providers listed below.
Inc. Canada

claim: House Speaker Nancy Pelosi said of US employers, “Employers cutting hours is a good thing. It then gives that person time to pursue their dreams and passions.”
label: Refuted
justification: Quote verification - Answer and source refute the claim.

questions.question: Did House Speaker Nancy Pelosi say, “Employers cutting hours is a good thing. It then gives that person time to pursue their dreams and passions.”?

answers.answer: No
answers.boolean_explanation: The quote seems to be misinterpreted from a statement Pelosi made in 2013 in a CNN interview titled "State of Union" where she said, “Overwhelmingly for the American people, this is a liberation,” she answered, in part. “This is life, healthy life, liberty, the freedom to pursue your happiness which could be, follow your passion for good rather than follow your palate and be constrained by your policy.” after being asked if she was worried that the Affordable Care Act would result in employers reducing employee hours to keep the costs of providing health care to employees down.

I am appalled, angry, shocked and some ‘media’ sources use me to make total fake headlines in the sensible subject of French current events and adding my religion and the French National Team to the pot,” Pogba posted on Instagram.

“I am taking legal action against the publishers and spreaders of these 100% Fake News,” he added.

questions.question: When was Ruth Bader Ginsburg nominated and confirmed?

answers.answer: President Clinton announced Ruth Bader Ginsburg as White's replacement on June 15, 1993, and she was confirmed by the United States Senate on August 3, 1993.

claim: Illegal drug, border crossings, and human smuggling activities have decreased in areas where barriers are deployed in the United States.
label: Not Enough Evidence
justification:
15 miles of new barriers seems unlikely to account for the halving of illegal crossing from 2019 to 2020. Particularly as 2019 is the outlier in the period 2018 to 2020.

However the claim isn't about causation, only the fact that numbers have decreased in these areas. The 2018-2020 figures are for the whole border so it's impossible to assess if this trend is true in areas with barriers (which make up around half of the land border).
speaker: Chase Jennings

questions.question: Which border is this claim referring to?

answers.answer: The US-Mexico border.

questions.question: Did the US-Mexico border have barriers before President Tump took office?

answers.answer: Yes
answers.boolean_explanation: About 654 miles of the 1,991-mile border already had some kind of fabricated barrier when Mr. Trump took office. Much of the rest has natural barriers; approximately 1,295 miles of the border are river, and 696 miles are land.

answers.answer: Yes
answers.boolean_explanation: Before he took office, there were 654 miles (just over 1,000km) of barrier along the southern border - made up of 354 miles of barricades to stop pedestrians and 300 miles of anti-vehicle fencing.

questions.question: How much of the border has barriers as of October 2020?

answers.answer:
Now, according to US Customs and Border Protection (CBP) in its 6 October status report, the southern border has 669 miles of "primary barrier" - the first structure people heading from Mexico to the US will encounter - and 65 miles of "secondary barrier" - which usually runs behind the primary structure as a further obstacle.

This means that in areas where no barricades existed before, they have built 15 miles of new, primary barrier or "border wall system", as it is called by CBP.

​​About a further 350 miles of barrier has been built, according to CBP, made up of replacement structures and some new secondary barrier.

More is planned, too, with 378 miles of new and replacement barrier either under construction or in the "pre-construction phase". Less than half of this will be in locations where no barriers currently exist, according to CBP.

questions.question: How many border encounters were there in 2018-2020?
When did the Covid-19 pandemic emerge?

answers.answer: The novel coronavirus (SARS-CoV-2) that causes COVID-19 first emerged in the Chinese city of Wuhan in 2019 and was declared a pandemic by the World Health Organization (WHO)

claim: US President Trump issued an executive order for the White House to take over all electrical grids, which will include internet servers, broadcasting systems and electronic systems
label: Refuted
justification: An order to secure the bulk power system was misquoted as being an order to take over all electrical grids.

questions.question: Who has control over all the electrical grids?

answers.answer:
Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) imposes mandatory reliability standards on all generation and transmission owners that tie into power grid. FERC has authorized North American Electric Reliability Corporation to implement its standards.

North American Electric Reliability Corporation (NERC) delegates its authority to 8 regional reliability councils (as of June 2018). These councils audit all participants (transmission owners, generation owners, and power grid operators) in their region to assure the standards are being followed.

questions.question: Does the White House control electrical grids, including internet servers, broadcasting systems and electronic systems?

answers.answer: No
answers.boolean_explanation: Every state in USA has a commission that ensures the Owners are delivering safe and reliable electric power to its constituents. This oversight is in addition to the federal oversight.

questions.question: Did Donald Trump issue an executive order to take over all electrical grids, which will include internet servers, broadcasting systems and electronic systems?

answers.answer: No
answers.boolean_explanation:
The president did issue an executive order intended to secure the country’s bulk-power system, but the order does not allow the White House to “take over” electrical grids.

The order seeks to protect the U.S.’ electricity system from cyber and other attacks, Reuters reported.

claim: US president Joe Biden (candidate at the time of claim) supports the new green deal (The Green New Deal is a nonbinding resolution, introduced by Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez and Sen. Ed Markey last year, to tackle climate change.)
label: Conflicting Evidence/Cherrypicking
justification: Biden did not openly say that he supports the new green deal but his plans aligns with the new green deal
speaker: Common Sense Voters Of America LLC

questions.question: Does U.S Presidential candidate  Joe Biden support the Green New Deal resolution introduced by Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez and Sen. Ed Markey in 2019

answers.answer: If you set aside the most left-wing programs included in New York Representative Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez’s 2019 Green New Deal resolution, like Medicare for All and a federal job guarantee, Biden’s plans broadly align with an approach advocated by the green new deal.

questions.question: What are Joe Biden's environmental policies?

answers.answer: Part of Biden’s “Build Back Better” agenda, these plans are a Green New Deal in all but name. If you set aside the most attention-grabbing left-wing programs included in New York Representative Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez’s 2019 Green New Deal resolution, like Medicare for All and a federal job guarantee, Biden’s plans broadly align with an approach advocated by the left-wing of the Democratic party. Firstly, like the Green New Deal, Biden’s plans reframe climate action as a jobs, infrastructure and clean energy stimulus.

questions.question: Is Medicare for all included in the green new deal?

answers.answer: Yes
answers.boolean_explanation: Medicare for all was in the Green New Deal.

questions.question: Does Biden support Medicare for All?

answers.answer: Biden railed against the Trump administration's countless attacks on the Affordable Care Act. As president, he promised to protect and build on the ACA. Although he wants to ensure healthcare is a right and not a privilege, he does not support Medicare-for-all or eliminating private insurance because it would mean getting rid of Obamacare and starting over on political negotiations.

claim: A mail-in ballot application for voting in the US presidential election which was sent to a deceased grandfather in the State of New Mexico is evidence of potential widespread voter fraud via mail-in ballots.
label: Refuted
justification: There is ample evidence provided that there was not widespread voter fraud.
speaker: Don Larkin

questions.question: What are some ways that mail-in ballots are protected from fraud?

answers.answer: Only valid voters can get a ballot in the mail

It is very hard to make fake ballots and mail-ballot envelopes.
Ballots feature particular design elements that are difficult to copy.
It’s very hard to duplicate mail-ballot envelopes
In general, voters cannot return their absentee ballots in just any old envelope. Rather, the ballots must be returned inside an official government envelope, which is typically mailed directly to voters along with the ballot itself.

Voters must affirm their identity
Every state has a process for verifying that absentee voters...
In most states, voters must sign the outside of their return envelope. This signature usually cannot deviate significantly from the signature on their original voter registration card, or the ballot will be rejected. If anything, the process of matching these signatures is often too strict, critics say, leading election administrators to unfairly reject authentic votes from registered voters due to slight signature discrepancies.

questions.question: How does the US Postal Service assist to detect fraudulent ballots?

answers.answer: The Postal Service coordinates with state and local election departments to flag any ballots that deviate in even minor ways from typical procedure.

questions.question: Is voter fraud a serious crime in the US?

answers.answer:
Voter fraud is a serious federal and state crime
For a federal election, each act of voter fraud can be punished by five years in prison and a US$10,000 fine, plus any additional state penalties.

By contrast, all that work put into attempting fraud would net the perpetrator only a single vote in favor of their preferred candidate. As a report from the Brennan Center for Justice succinctly put it, “That single extra vote is simply not worth the price.”

questions.question: Why was a mail-in ballot application sent to a deceased person in the State of New Mexico?

answers.answer: In June 2020, due to the COVID-19 pandemic, a Senate Bill was signed, allowing mail-in applications to be sent automatically to all voters for their convenience.

claim: The PHE has began to combine Covid-19 and influenza statistics to make the numbers look worse.
label: Refuted
justification: Although COVID-19 and influenza numbers are in the same PHE document, they are not combined. Therefore, the claim is refuted.
speaker: Julia Hartley-Brewer

questions.question: Are the Covid-19 and influenza statistics published in the same document?

answers.answer: Yes
answers.boolean_explanation: The PHE document documents has influenza and Covid-19 numbers in the same document

questions.question: Are the Covid-19 and influenza numbers combined together in PHE statistics?

answers.answer: No
answers.boolean_explanation: The PHE document does not combine influenza and Covid-19 statistics

questions.question: Does putting them in the same document, have any effect on the COVID-19 statistics themselves, or change the numbers about either disease?

answers.answer: No
answers.boolean_explanation: The new report does contain combined data on the number of “acute respiratory infection incidents” (two or more lab confirmed cases or a suspected outbreak) reported by PHE Health Protection Teams, just as previous reports did before. These are presented as a total figure, before being broken down to show the figure for Covid-19 specifically.
